com: Formal offer by means of scheme of arrangement announced at 590p per share plus 22 pence dividend. 99.9% of votes were in favour at meetings on 16 April 2026.

Recommended final cash offer by private equity firm EQT at £60 per share. The offer will be implemented by means of a Court-sanctioned scheme of arrangement and is expected to become effective in Q4 2026 or Q1 2027.
Scheme of arrangement made at same terms as indicative proposal. Beazley shareholder vote passed on 22 April 2026. Expected to become effective during H2 2026.
Shareholders have overwhelmingly approved the scheme, but still subject to regulatory approval. Completion expected some time in Q3.

Formal offer (scheme) announced on 7 April 2026 by Tinicum and Blackstone at 297.85p in cash. Overwhelmingly approved by shareholders on 26 May 2026. Indicative offer of 270p from Advent was rejected. Advent withdrew on 8 April 2026. Arcline Investment Management had made an approach before withdrawing on 1 April 2026.

Indicative offer by Toscafund (which already owns 24.36%) at 250p per share, with unlisted share alternative, which Board is "minded to recommend". Approaches had been previously received from Bridgepoint and Triton, following a strategic review but talks ended.

In preliminary discussions with a number of interested counterparties. Potential offerors not required to be identified, but Providence Equity Partners was named on 13 May 2026 (PUSU deadline 10 June). Epiris and separate Oakley Capital/Giacom consortium named on 15 May 2026 but Oakley Capital/Giacom withdrew on 12 June 2026. On 10 June 2026, Providence & Epiris formed a consortium with a PUSU deadline of 8 July 2026.

Strategic review after meetings with financial investors who wanted to maximise value.  Fattal Hotels had made a pre-conditional proposal of £22 but withdrew on 19 June 2026. On 31 May 2026 PPHE received an indicative proposal from another interested party. No PUSU deadline.
Indicative all share offer by Acceler8 Ventures plc, valued at £600m. Offer subject to due diligence. Board "minded to recommend" and undertakings in respect of 24.59%. We expect a formal offer here.
Shareholders approved scheme on 11 March. Still subject to regulatory approval, with parties aiming to complete by end of Q2 2026.
Drax makes formal offer on 1 June 2026, by means of scheme. Offer follows strategic review and formal sales process.

b: LONDONMETRIC PROPERTY & SCHRODER REAL ESTATE INVESTMENT TRUST
com: LondonMetric Property PLC and Schroder REIT make indicative all share offer, after strategic review. 46% in LondonMetric shares and 54% in Schroder REIT shares. As proposal is part of formal sale process, no PUSU deadline.

Helios consortium (which already owns 45.11%) has gone hostile, with 85p formal offer. StoneX had made an indicative offer of 110p, before withdrawing on 5 May, given the Helios Consortium's lack of support.

Formal £235.2m offer by Charterhouse Capital, 41.7% undertakings. Scheme of arrangement in cash with a rollover alternative. Approved by shareholders on 8 June 2026. Effective date expected to be 30 July 2026.
Offer, by scheme of arrangement, from Qatar-based bank. Approved by shareholders on 27 April 2026, and will become effective on 25 June 2026.

Multiple unsolicited proposals from Saudi Arabia's Alamadiyaf al-Masiyyah, which has due diligence access. No price given. Discussions were ongoing and the board was seeking clarity around Alamadiyaf al-Masiyyah's funding arrangements.  On 24 March 2026, Capricorn rejected proposal from Dragon Oil to acquire its assets in the Western Desert.
Recommended offer (scheme of arrangement) - expected to complete on 2 July 2026. Already owns 27.9% and undertakings at 12%. Note an offer by Exponent at 260p failed in November 2025.
Strategic review, has received approaches from two parties.
Strategic review & formal sale process. Has not received an indicative offer but has held preliminary discussions with a small number of counterparties.
Approved by target shareholders and FCA. Long stop date of 29 July 2026.
At “minded to recommend” stage. Due diligence at an advanced stage. We expect a formal offer.
Formal sale process. Discussions with potential bidders ongoing.
Discussions are with selected third parties as part of a private strategic review process.

Formal offer by NEO NEXT+ (scheme). Follows discussions with three parties, being (i) Capricorn Energy PLC ("Capricorn Energy"), (ii) Petrogas International E&P Coöperatief U.A., a subsidiary of Mohammed Al Barwani LLC, and (iii) Blue Concept Hld AS, all of which had withdrawn by June 2026. Previous scheme by Viaro Energy had lapsed on 31 March 2026.
Formal offer by means of scheme of arrangement. 48.9% undertakings. Completion expected Q3.

Formal cash offer (scheme) from US ingredients group Ingredion Inc, valuing each share at up to 615p - 595p in cash plus permitted final and interim dividends of up to 20p. Follows proposal at same price.
Mixed cash and share offer, with support of 50.14% of shareholders. Expected to complete during the third quarter of 2026.
Unsolicited offer from Glenstone REIT (which already owns 24%) at a price of 70p per share, rejected by target Board. The offer has an acceptance condition of 50%.
Unsolicited proposal from H.B. Fuller Company. Follows previous approach by TA Associates, which withdrew on 18 May 2026

Castlelake had made three unsolicited proposals to the Easyjet Board since 12 June 2026, the latest being 625p per share on 20 June 2026. All three proposals were rejected by the Easyjet Board which maintains its stance that the proposal is highly opportunistic, delivered against the backdrop of easyJet’s temporarily depressed share price, and still fundamentally undervaluing easyJet and its prospects.
Non-binding indicative offer, with no price. Due diligence and discussions ongoing.
